target: eval_cumulative_dense (Cumsum/Cumprod/Cummax/Cummin over dense F64/I64). The scan looped over outer_count independent lines serially; the per-element accumulator op (&impl Fn) does not inline, making it compute-bound (~10x slower than memory-bound for 4M f64).
lever: The outer lines of a cumulative scan are independent. For contiguous last-axis scans (axis_stride==1) that are large (>=1<<18) with many lines, write the dense output directly and split whole-line blocks across threads (scan_contiguous_lines_to_vec / scan_contiguous_lines_from). Forward serial scans push each output once (no clone); reverse / threaded fill an initialized buffer in disjoint line-blocks; one-line/small scans keep the clone+in-place path. Op closures gained a + Sync bound. NOTE: implementation developed in the shared working tree (collaborative across the agent swarm); landed here to prevent it being stranded uncommitted across context resets.
isomorphism: BIT-EXACT. dense_cumulative_bit_identical_to_literal_path (dense fast path == generic Literal path, all 4 ops x axis x reverse) and threaded_cumulative_matches_serial_reference (4096x128 >= 1<<18, threaded == independent per-line serial, cumsum/cumprod/cummax x forward/reverse). Lines are independent and per-line element order is unchanged, so threading cannot alter results. 1100 fj-lax tests pass.
decision: SHIP. 3.04x on the many-lines contiguous scan (the common ML case: cumulative over an inner axis of a [batch, seq, feat] tensor), zero regression (small/strided/single-line keep their prior path). Single-line scan is only 1.34x — it needs chunked parallel-prefix (Blelloch), filed as the follow-up.
follow_ups: Single-line / few-long-lines cumulative scan: chunked parallel-prefix (local scan per chunk in parallel -> serial scan of chunk totals -> parallel add offsets) to parallelize the dependency chain. Non-contiguous (strided) axis scans remain serial.
